See you next week I spose

tracks.title: insulation tape
tracks.description: Waking up 37 minutes before the deadline and then using the stems I already had open in Bitwig, no time for post-processing.. So just a quick one take jam without even getting out of bed. Now a little blog... I've got 4 weeks of Annual leave today --- My plan is to write/finish some tracks for a live set at a festival in a 'Secret Victorian Bush location, not too far from Melbourne' as well as work on some controller setups, midi/osc (kinect / resolume stuff) ...I'm also learning Unity Engine 5 so Lets see how it all goes in the next 4 weeks--- and maybe tie everything together...Maybe I can start working on syntax demo early for a change!

EpochJam (PixiTracker)
tracks.description: With >40 degree days and an on going heatwave in Melbourne, and needing a new pair of headphones (I think I've lost my good ones) - I've decided to go with a nuclear option. So here's a PixiTracker jam track! Cool application by Alexander Zolotov (A.K.A Nightradio) from warmplace.ru Check it -- http://www.warmplace.ru/soft/pixitracker/ Also available on Android (most nightradio software run on multiple platforms) (may run on your toaster!) Yep, Built this simple track in about 2 hours. No post FX on this one, just a simple native jam... because nuclear, because weeklybeats. The video output is also native from the PixiTracker software. / no post fx. Link to piximod file Next week is a colab with Hungee Funk ... My first Weeklybeats Colab for the year! - exciting

tracks.title: dem duty cycles from studio hell (MidiNES)
The Mission Objective
tracks.description: Thanks Weeklybeats 2012... I started late in the year, learned a lot about my own workflow. Missed many weeks here and there, but it definitely helped! Were still alive! I hope there's a weeklybeats 2013, I'm keen to try and get it down for every week.. (Or every month!?) (or every fortnight!) but definately want to keep a patern of consistency up.. I noticed a few of you might not be up for WeeklyBeats 2013 (according to the forum) so I'd like to thank everyone who made this site what it is, It's been a nightmare and a dream at the same time.. I'm going to hit refresh on 1/1/2013 00:00 -- Hope there's new site I think i'm looking forward to 2013 in many ways...

tracks.title: Advance Apocalyptic Australian
tracks.description: Kinda rushed it... Kinda didn't put the limiters in properly. (Sorry if its a bit heavy in parts on the peaks) It's kinda 3am. Were all gonna die, ... eventually This weeks beat is Apocalyptically inspired

tracks.title: RaspberryPI powered Simple MIDI Experiments
tracks.description: The Video - http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=75M-e1nfJfY Just a proof of concept, Audio excerpt from my Raspberry Pi sequencer demo thingy, (I just threw FX into it in post so it wouldn't sound too boring) Raspberry Pi powered MIDI Sequencer and Clock sync master. Software: SEQ24 / Wheezy Debian / on a Raspberry PI Hardware: MidiNES / Korg ESX / USB Midi adapters (http://dev.aday.net.au/random/midiadapters.jpg) Yeah, not much, but everything works.. Video here: http://youtu.be/75M-e1nfJfY

tracks.title: Syntax Obnoxiouscore
tracks.description: Hi! The first part of this was constructed at Syntax Demoparty 2012 for lulz ... -- Samples from laptop microphone from random people in the room, including my self/mudlord/cTrix saying whatever! -- Sampled Google Text to Speech (which was originally Voice to Text <- and then -> Text to voice again) and it got it mostly all wrong but with weird results ... -- Prizes of admiration and respect for those who can make out wtf is going on here... -- ... been awake for over 20 hours ...

(started at the 2hour to go mark) (mixed out and i guess 'finished' at the 11 minutes to go mark) (Thanks to PhilHarmonic for the reupload link (mine died at the 30 seconds to go mark!!!) Was initially inspired by the fog I woke up too in Melbourne, Australia

tracks.title: Wake up and make BRKFast (LSDJ) vs (ESX)
tracks.description: I had way bigger plan's for this week's weeklybeat, but my gameboy batteries died and It was really late last night. So I recorded a few phrases I wrote this week and passed it though my Electribe ESX an hour before deadline! It's a bit rough in places, so a few audition FX'es to fix the flow a little. I basically beta-tested though cTrix's LSDJ tutorial which finally filled out some gaps I had with my LSDJ knowledge. Great success! (Check out the tutorial when he uploads it, Awesome comprehensive) <- Cheers dude! UPDATE: cTrix has posted said video ! --> http://vimeo.com/47192618 <-- Chip on!

tracks.title: Chillstep?
tracks.description: I don't think I've spent this long in one session writing a complete track for quite some time now, feels good. Hope this turns out ok. I think I loose a lot of the production when I mix things down and master it in audacity, things get lost.. Made the piano bit by hand, no quantize (except cleaned up velocity's and start points on a few here and there) Who cares? well, I usually Q-Rec.... meh I dunno, I do know if I didn't have work tomorrow I'd probably keep messing with this for another few hours... but run the risk of potentially breaking it somehow. So I think I'll mark this, COMPLETE for week 30! ... yay! I wish I made it longer... meh Update Edit: I made this track longer! Released on Soundcloud - http://soundcloud.com/adaynetau/diving-into-a-fractal

tracks.title: 2012am (arpeggiated sequencers and you)
tracks.description: I finished this track at 2:12am (EST+10) (7 hours before the deadline) --== arpeggiated sequencers and you ==-- need to sleep... hope to write something tomorrow!

tracks.title: SynthAnteMeridiem synthesizer jam for brekky
tracks.description: Wrote this in only a few takes this morning ... I'm not too happy with the levels, it drops levels at the end and I'm still sleepy... Wrote it still in bed too, not very comfortable lugging a 49Key MIDI Controller and trying to stay comfortable. morning synthesizer jam for brekky

tracks.title: 6 month neurosis problem solver
tracks.description: I made this early!! It's Friday the 6th of July... around 7 A.M. I have a OCD thing for patterns... and I broke the previous 6 months of trying to make a beat every week ... because i was being neurotic about it! And I really want to fill the solid 6 month block for the remainder of this year.. And so after a bit of reflection I came to the conclusion that the obvious solution is to WRITE THE BEAT BEFORE DEADLINE (NOT THE NIGHT BEFORE) So officially, Friday (Or Wednesday is my new precieved deadline) I hope this is the begining of my horrible procrastination ways of the second half of 2012. Happy Winter Solstice everyone... I wrote this druing the friday morning sunrise off my balcony, freezing cold.. near my plants... 'I have frequently seen people become neurotic when they content themselves with inadequate or wrong answers to the questions of life (Jung, [1961] 1989:140).'
